Rust · Python · WebAssembly

chess-corners-rs

High-performance chessboard-corner detection in Rust. Detects X-junctions — points where four alternating dark and bright cells meet — with subpixel accuracy. Two detector families: ChESS ring response and Radon ray-sum response. Ships as Rust crates, a Python package, and a WebAssembly module.

Guide

The mdBook: algorithms, the ChESS and Radon pipeline internals, orientation methods, and a getting-started tutorial.

API reference

Generated cargo doc for the workspace crates — the facade, the core, and optional ML refiner.

Demo

Detect corners in your browser via WebAssembly. Load a sample image and tune detector parameters live.

Performance

Per-image, per-stage detection timings on sample boards — where the milliseconds go, measured rather than claimed.

Source on GitHub · vitavision.dev
